senses.definition: to rest one's mind and body, with one's eyes closed

examples.x: I had a long day at work, so I'm going to hit the sack early tonight.
After the late-night party, it was a relief to finally hit the sack and get some sleep.
When I'm feeling tired, I know it's time to hit the sack and recharge for the next day.
He decided to hit the sack and get a good night's sleep before the big presentation.
During the camping trip, we would hit the sack as soon as it got dark to make the most of the early morning activities.
After the thunderstorm, I didn't sleep a wink because of the loud noise.
She had so much on her mind that she couldn't sleep a wink before the important exam.
The baby was teething, so the parents didn't sleep a wink as they comforted their little one throughout the night.
The excitement of the trip ahead was so overwhelming that I didn't sleep a wink the night before.
The unexpected car alarm kept the entire neighborhood awake, and no one slept a wink until it was finally turned off.

senses.definition: to manage to get a full sleep without anyone or anything interrupting

examples.x: I have to set numerous alarms for the morning because I sleep like a log every night!
After a long day of hiking, he slept like a baby in the comfortable cabin.
The rainstorm was so relaxing that I slept like a log throughout the night.
Despite the noise from the nearby construction, she managed to sleep like a baby with earplugs.

senses.definition: to constlantly turn over to the sides due to having difficulty sleeping

examples.x: I tossed and turned all night.
The heat wave made it impossible to sleep, and he tossed and turned all night trying to cool down.
When dealing with a personal crisis, he often found himself tossing and turning, unable to find peace.
After drinking too much coffee in the evening, he spent the night tossing and turning with caffeine-induced restlessness.

senses.definition: to manage to get some sleep
sleeping so soundly that one cannot be easily awakened

examples.x: After pulling an all-nighter to meet the deadline, I was down for the count and needed a long nap.
The flu hit me hard, and I was down for the count, spending most of the weekend in bed.
Following the intense workout, my muscles were sore, and I felt down for the count, struggling to get up from the couch.
The long-haul flight left me feeling completely drained, and I was down for the count when I arrived at my destination.
She had been working nonstop for days, and by the time the weekend came, she was down for the count, barely able to get out of bed.

senses.definition: a short, light nap or brief sleep taken to rest and regain energy

examples.x: During the lunch break, I like to take forty winks in my office to recharge for the afternoon.
After the long road trip, I pulled over at a rest area and took forty winks to feel more refreshed.
The conference was exhausting, so I found a quiet corner to take a quick forty winks.
He had a hectic schedule, but he managed to squeeze in forty winks on the train ride home.
Whenever I'm feeling tired and need a quick energy boost, I'll take forty winks before continuing my work.
After the exhausting hike, I pounded my ear the moment I hit the bed.
The tranquil sounds of the ocean helped me pound my ear during our beach vacation.
When the power went out and the room was pitch black, I pounded my ear and slept through the night.
The comfortable mattress and cozy blankets made it easy for me to pound my ear without interruption.
Despite the loud thunderstorm outside, I pounded my ear and didn't wake up until morning.

senses.definition: to get in bed for sleeping

examples.x: After a long day of gardening, she was ready to hit the hay and get a good night's rest.
The early morning flight meant I had to hit the hay early the night before.
When the camping trip ended, we couldn't wait to hit the hay in our comfortable sleeping bags.
The children were so tired after playing outside that they were eager to hit the hay as soon as the sun set.

senses.definition: someone who has a tendency to get up early every morning

examples.x: She's always been an early bird, waking up at the crack of dawn to start her day.
The early birds at the farmers' market get the freshest produce.
As an early bird, he enjoys the quiet and peaceful mornings for his daily meditation.
To avoid traffic, he's become an early bird and commutes to work before rush hour.
The conference starts at 8 AM, so all the early birds will get the best seats in the front row.

senses.definition: used to tell someone it is time to get up and get out of bed

examples.x: Rise and shine, sleepy head!
The alarm clock rang, and my mom called out, "Time to rise and shine, it's a new day!"
After a restful night's sleep, I opened the curtains and welcomed the morning with a "rise and shine" attitude.
As the camp counselor, I woke up the kids with a cheerful "rise and shine" to start the day's activities.
Despite the early hour, she embraced the "rise and shine" spirit, looking forward to the adventures of the day.

senses.definition: a person who has a tendency to sleep very late at night

examples.x: She has always been a night owl, working on her creative projects late into the night.
Being a night owl, he finds it easier to concentrate and study when the house is quiet.
Night owls often enjoy the tranquility of the late hours, when they can work or read without interruptions.
As a writer, she's most inspired and productive as a night owl, crafting stories when the world sleeps.
The night owl lifestyle allows for peaceful reflection, and some people find it conducive to their personal creativity and productivity.

word_list.word: in the land of the living

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who is completely awake and conscious

examples.x: As the sun rose over the horizon, Mark emerged from his deep slumber and joined the rest of his family in the living room, finally in the land of the living.
Sarah woke up from a deep sleep and sat up in bed, finally back in the land of the living.

senses.definition: (of a person) in a state of deep unconsciousness or sleep, typically due to extreme fatigue

examples.x: After the exhausting hike, I was out like a light as soon as I hit the bed.
The anesthesia took effect, and he was out like a light before the surgery began.
The long day's work left him drained, and he was out like a light in his recliner.
The warm bath and cozy blankets made me out like a light in no time, enjoying a deep and restful sleep.
After the long hike, I slept like a log, not waking up until morning.
The soothing lullaby helped the baby sleep like a log throughout the night.
He was so exhausted that he slept like a log, undisturbed by the noise outside.
The comfortable mattress and cool room temperature made me sleep like a log.
After the busy workweek, I needed to sleep like a log during the weekend to recharge.
